title: Inspector Error Handling, Security & Documentation
goal: Harden the inspector system against failures (inspector model timeouts, malformed responses, empty outputs), enforce credential safety in inspector prompts and UI, and update documentation to cover inspector features.

in_scope: Ensure inspector model call failures show non-blocking error status — local checks still render
Handle invalid JSON with required fields configured: return 'cannot evaluate fields' state
Handle empty output responses: return structured failure summary rather than raw exception
Verify API keys are not embedded in inspector prompts or displayed in inspector failure messages
Verify inspector prompts avoid embedding secrets from runtime configuration
Add timeout handling for inspector model calls
Ensure inspector failures do not block arena results rendering
Update README.md with inspector feature documentation: flags, workflow, check types, limitations
out_of_scope: Retry logic for failed inspector calls

inputs: All inspector module code from Parts 01-05
PRD Week 2 section 5.3 — Advanced features and edge cases
outputs: src/app.py — inspector-specific error display in UI
dependencies: 2_output_inspector/Parts 01-05 — all inspector features must be implemented
checklist: In inspector.py, wrap all semantic check calls in try/except and return error result dict on failure
Add timeout parameter to semantic inspector calls (default: 30 seconds)
Handle inspector model timeout: return status='error' with detail 'Inspector model timed out'
Handle inspector model returning non-JSON: attempt fallback parsing, then return error status
In check_required_fields, when JSON is invalid return 'not_evaluated' for all fields with clear reason
For empty model output (None or empty string), return structured fail summary for all checks
Audit inspector prompts: ensure no API keys, endpoints, or secrets are embedded
Audit inspector UI: ensure no credential values appear in error messages or badges
In app.py, ensure inspector failures display as non-blocking warnings (st.warning) — arena results remain visible
Ensure local structural checks execute and render even when semantic checks fail
Update README.md: add Inspector System section with flag descriptions, check types, and usage workflow
Document inspector limitations: heuristic Markdown validation, single-call per response, no persistence
acceptance_criteria: Semantic call failures show non-blocking error status in inspection panel
Local structural checks still execute and render when semantic checks fail
Invalid JSON with required fields returns 'Not Evaluated' state per field
Empty output returns structured failure summary rather than raw exception
API keys are not displayed in inspector UI or failure messages
Inspector prompts avoid embedding secrets from runtime configuration
Inspector failures do not block arena results rendering
README.md documents inspector features, flags, and limitations
review_focus: All error paths produce user-friendly messages (no stack traces visible)
Security audit covers inspector prompt construction and error message rendering
Local-vs-semantic check independence is maintained under all failure scenarios
README documentation matches actual inspector behavior and flags
Timeout values are reasonable for inspector model calls
risks_or_notes: Inspector model may be the same deployment used for comparison — concurrent failures would affect both
Non-JSON inspector responses are a likely edge case — robust parsing fallback is critical
Documentation should caution that semantic checks add latency proportional to model count
Edge case testing ideally requires a live inspector model or realistic mocks
definition_of_done: All inspector error paths are handled gracefully without exceptions reaching the user
Credential safety is verified across inspector prompts and UI
Local checks render independently of semantic check failures
Manual testing confirms inspector happy-path and all failure scenarios
App stability is maintained under inspector failure conditions
